sourceMovie.title: Mary Poppins
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mary Poppins works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Addams Family Values
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mary Poppins clicked for you, Addams Family Values (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Nanny McPhee
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mary Poppins clicked for you, Nanny McPhee (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mary Poppins?
faq.answer: Try Addams Family Values, Nanny McPhee, Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical—each shares DNA with Mary Poppins while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mary Poppins?
faq.answer: Mary Poppins (1964) is best described as Comedy, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mary Poppins part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Mary Poppins Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
